pokemon:dodrio.name: Dodrio
pokemon:dodrio.summary: Apparently, the heads aren’t the only parts of the body that Dodrio has three of. It has three sets of hearts and lungs as well, so it is capable of running long distances without rest.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Apparently, the heads aren’t the only parts of the body that Dodrio has three of. It has three sets of hearts and lungs as well, so it is capable of running long distances without rest.
When Doduo evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.
When Doduo evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.
Uses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head stays awake.
An enemy that takes its eyes off any of the three heads--even for a second--will get pecked severely.
When DODUO evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.
A peculiar POKéMON species with three heads. It vigorously races across grassy plains even in arid seasons with little rainfall.
An odd species that is rarely found. The three heads respectively represent joy, sadness, and anger.
It collects data and plans three times as wisely, but it may think too much and be­ come immobilized.
It collects data and plans three times as wisely, but it may think too much and fall into a state of immobility.
Uses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head is said to stay awake.
One of Doduo’s two heads splits to form a unique species. It runs close to 40 mph in prairies.
One of Doduo’s two heads splits to form a unique species. It runs close to 40 mph in prairies.
Watch out if Dodrio’s three heads are looking in three separate directions. It’s a sure sign that it is on its guard. Don’t go near this Pokémon if it’s being wary—it may decide to peck you.
When DODUO evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.
When DODUO evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.
Uses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head stays awake.
Watch out if DODRIO’s three heads are looking in three separate directions. It’s a sure sign that it is on its guard. Don’t go near this POKéMON if it’s being wary - it may decide to peck you.
Apparently, the heads aren’t the only parts of the body that DODRIO has three of. It has three sets of hearts and lungs as well, so it is capable of running long distances without rest.
If one of the heads gets to eat, the others will be satisfied, too, and they will stop squabbling.
If one of the heads gets to eat, the others will be satisfied, too, and they will stop squabbling.
When Doduo evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.
When Doduo evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.
An enemy that takes its eyes off any of the three heads--even for a second--will get pecked severely.
An odd species that is rarely found. The three heads respectively represent joy, sadness, and anger.
One of DODUO's 2 heads splits to form a unique species. It runs close to 40 MPH in prairies.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Apparently, the heads aren’t the only parts of the body that Dodrio has three of. It has three sets of hearts and lungs as well, so it is capable of running long distances without rest.

pokemon:doduo.name: Doduo
pokemon:doduo.summary: Doduo’s two heads contain completely identical brains. A scientific study reported that on rare occasions, there will be examples of this Pokémon possessing different sets of brains.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Doduo’s two heads contain completely identical brains. A scientific study reported that on rare occasions, there will be examples of this Pokémon possessing different sets of brains.
The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.
The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.
A bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.
It lives on a grassy plain where it can see a long way. If it sees an enemy, it runs away at 60 mph.
The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.
Even while eating or sleeping, one of the heads remains always vigilant for any sign of danger. When threatened, it flees at over 60 miles per hour.
A two-headed POKéMON that was discovered as a sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.
By alternately raising and lower­ ing its two heads, it balances itself to be more stable while running.
By alternately raising and lowering its two heads, it balances itself to be more stable while running.
A bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.
Its short wings make flying difficult. Instead, this Pokémon runs at high speed on developed legs.
Its short wings make flying difficult. Instead, this Pokémon runs at high speed on developed legs.
Doduo’s two heads never sleep at the same time. Its two heads take turns sleeping, so one head can always keep watch for enemies while the other one sleeps.
The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.
The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.
A bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.
DODUO’s two heads never sleep at the same time. Its two heads take turns sleeping, so one head can always keep watch for enemies while the other one sleeps.
DODUO’s two heads contain completely identical brains. A scientific study reported that on rare occasions, there will be examples of this POKéMON possessing different sets of brains.
It races through grassy plains with powerful strides, leaving footprints up to four inches deep.
It races through grassy plains with powerful strides, leaving footprints up to four inches deep.
The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.
The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.
A two-headed Pokémon that was discovered as a sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.
The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.
Its short wings make flying dif­ ficult. Instead, this POKéMON runs at high speed on developed legs.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Doduo’s two heads contain completely identical brains. A scientific study reported that on rare occasions, there will be examples of this Pokémon possessing different sets of brains.
